summary:
- Ruby Core team has taken over maintenance of RubyGems and Bundler, ending a period of uncertainty and restoring trust in the ecosystem.  
- The move was framed as a return to stability, but it leaves open the question of why the previous maintainers were removed and whether the community will ever get a full explanation.  
- The incident has reignited the debate over centralized vs. decentralized package distribution, with gem.coop emerging as a new, community-driven alternative.  
- The episode has exposed a governance and transparency deficit in the Ruby ecosystem, leaving many to question who really controls the infrastructure that the community depends on.
